audit_principle: When evidence and narrative disagree, the narrative changes—not the evidence.
negative_findings: No measurable episodic-learning lift on sealed authoritative STRICT trio (mean_episodic_lift=0.0).
This is evidence, not a benchmark or protocol failure.
No publishable claim may imply positive episodic lift on this evidence base.

cold_read.criterion: Could a technically competent reviewer, unfamiliar with the drafting history, infer this claim solely from the cited evidence?

claims.text: Experimental benchmarks comparing GRAG with conventional RAG show improvements in retrieval quality on a hardened goal-disambiguation evaluation suite (see §4; canonical 2026-03-14 run).
These results are retrieval-metric evidence under the stated corpus and case mix—not a claim of agent-level task-completion or episodic-learning lift.
GRAG introduces a directional retrieval signal goal_embedding − current_state_embedding and scores documents with query + directional similarity.
GRAG introduces adaptive blending, dynamically adjusting α based on task progress.
GRAG integrates a graph-structured memory layer; hybrid score 0.7 vector + 0.3 graph connectivity (prototype).
Dynamic graph edge learning is operational via GraphRefiner; metrics logged in GragDiagnostics / grag_benchmark.jsonl.
Canonical 2026-03-14: Precision@5 +0.036, MRR +0.071, nDCG@5 +0.041 (RAG vs GRAG).
Goal-disambiguation bucket: +0.202 nDCG@5 over baseline RAG (same run).
Early 100-chunk sandbox (2026-03-09) showed +0.200 mean nDCG@5 — not representative of hardened suites.
Goal-aware retrieval improved ranked-retrieval metrics on the stated evaluation suite, especially on the goal-disambiguation bucket.
GRAG demonstrates that integrating [directional vectors, adaptive weighting, graph relationships] can improve retrieval performance in scenarios where standard semantic search is insufficient.

citation_anchor.note: Suite-scoped metrics only; audited sentence lacked suite bound

claims.revised_text: On the stated §4 evaluation suite, integrating goal-relative directional vectors, adaptive retrieval weighting, and graph-structured relationships improved ranked-retrieval metrics relative to baseline RAG, especially where goal context disambiguates the query.

claims.text: Whether these retrieval-metric gains translate to agent task-completion efficiency remains future work (§6).
Preliminary benchmarks demonstrate improved retrieval performance in goal-disambiguation scenarios under the stated evaluation suite (§4).
Broader claims about the role of goal-aware retrieval in future agent architectures remain outside the present evidence.
By integrating [GRAG mechanisms], GRAG extends traditional retrieval-augmented systems to better support multi-step reasoning tasks.

citation_anchor.note: Architecture exists; agent-level 'better support' not demonstrated

claims.revised_text: By integrating goal-relative directional retrieval, adaptive blending of retrieval signals, and graph-structured knowledge memory, GRAG extends traditional retrieval-augmented systems with mechanisms intended to support multi-step, goal-directed retrieval. Agent-level task-completion benefits remain future work (§6).

claims.text: Episodic memory improves learning / positive episodic lift under live authoritative STRICT on the v1.2 trio.
Phase D establishes trust in evaluation machinery; Phase E establishes trust in empirical claims made using that machinery.
After EP-01.5 and sync repairs, authoritative harness produced reproducible results with no instrumentation defects; lift=0.0 is an observed outcome, not a benchmark failure.
